      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.effectiveTime",
        "extension" : [
          {
            "url" : "http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/elementdefinition-defaulttype",
            "valueCanonical" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/SXCM-TS"
          }
        ],
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.effectiveTime",
        "representation" : [
          "typeAttr"
        ],
        "slicing" : {
          "discriminator" : [
            {
              "type" : "pattern",
              "path" : "operator"
            }
          ],
          "rules" : "open"
        },
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "*",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.effectiveTime",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "*"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/SXCM-TS"
          },
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/IVL-TS"
          },
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/EIVL-TS"
          },
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/PIVL-TS"
          },
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/SXPR-TS"
          }
        ],
        "mustSupport" : true
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.effectiveTime:effectiveTimeDuree",
        "extension" : [
          {
            "url" : "http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/elementdefinition-defaulttype",
            "valueCanonical" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/SXCM-TS"
          }
        ],
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.effectiveTime",
        "representation" : [
          "typeAttr"
        ],
        "sliceName" : "effectiveTimeDuree",
        "short" : "Durée de traitement",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.effectiveTime",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "*"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/SXCM-TS"
          },
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/IVL-TS"
          },
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/EIVL-TS"
          },
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/PIVL-TS"
          },
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/SXPR-TS"
          }
        ],
        "constraint" : [
          {
            "key" : "fr-operator-not-a-prescrit",
            "severity" : "error",
            "human" : "operator doit être différent de A",
            "expression" : "operator != 'A'",
            "source" : "https://interop.esante.gouv.fr/ig/document/core/StructureDefinition/fr-cda-traitement-prescrit"
          },
          {
            "key" : "fr-effectiveTime-duree-type-prescrit",
            "severity" : "error",
            "human" : "La durée doit être IVL-TS",
            "expression" : "is(IVL_TS)",
            "source" : "https://interop.esante.gouv.fr/ig/document/core/StructureDefinition/fr-cda-traitement-prescrit"
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.effectiveTime:effectiveTimeFrequence",
        "extension" : [
          {
            "url" : "http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/elementdefinition-defaulttype",
            "valueCanonical" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/SXCM-TS"
          }
        ],
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.effectiveTime",
        "representation" : [
          "typeAttr"
        ],
        "sliceName" : "effectiveTimeFrequence",
        "short" : "Fréquence d'administration",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "*",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.effectiveTime",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "*"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/SXCM-TS"
          },
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/IVL-TS"
          },
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/EIVL-TS"
          },
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/PIVL-TS"
          },
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/SXPR-TS"
          }
        ],
        "constraint" : [
          {
            "key" : "fr-operator-a-prescrit",
            "severity" : "error",
            "human" : "operator doit être A",
            "expression" : "operator = 'A'",
            "source" : "https://interop.esante.gouv.fr/ig/document/core/StructureDefinition/fr-cda-traitement-prescrit"
          },
          {
            "key" : "fr-effectiveTime-frequence-types-prescrit",
            "severity" : "error",
            "human" : "La fréquence doit être SXCM-TS, PIVL-TS, EIVL-TS ou SXPR-TS",
            "expression" : "is(TS) or is(PIVL_TS) or is(EIVL_TS) or is(SXPR_TS)",
            "source" : "https://interop.esante.gouv.fr/ig/document/core/StructureDefinition/fr-cda-traitement-prescrit"
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.priorityCode",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.priorityCode",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.priorityCode",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"

          }
        ],
        "binding" : {
          "strength" : "example",
          "valueSet" : "http://terminology.hl7.org/ValueSet/v3-ActPriority"
        }
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.repeatNumber",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.repeatNumber",
        "short" : "Nombre de renouvellement(s) possible(s): \r\n\n- Si aucun renouvellement autorisé (dispensation unique) : <repeatNumber value='0'/>\n- Si le renouvellement n'est pas limité (par ex : si une période de renouvellement est définie), la valeur est fixée à nullFlavor='NI'.",
        "min" : 1,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.repeatNumber",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/IVL-INT"
          }
        ],
        "mustSupport" : true
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.routeCode",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.routeCode",
        "short" : "Voie d'administration : \r\n\nDans le cas où la posologie n'est pas structurée et décrite uniquement dans la partie narrative, cet élément ne doit pas être présent.   \nDans le cas où la posologie est structurée, si la voie d'administration est connue, elle peut être indiquée à partir de la terminologie EDQM (0.4.0.127.0.16.1.1.2.1).      \nSi elle n'est pas connue, la raison pour laquelle elle est inconnue peut être décrite en utilisant l'attribut nullFlavor.",
        "definition" : "Voie d'administration",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.routeCode",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/CE"
          }
        ],
        "mustSupport" : true,
        "binding" : {
          "strength" : "example",
          "valueSet" : "http://terminology.hl7.org/ValueSet/v3-RouteOfAdministration"
        }
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.routeCode.nullFlavor",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.routeCode.nullFlavor",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],

        ],

      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.routeCode.originalText.integrityCheck",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.routeCode.originalText.integrityCheck",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Integrity Check",
        "definition" : "The integrity check is a short binary value representing a cryptographically strong checksum that is calculated over the binary data. The purpose of this property, when communicated with a reference is for anyone to validate later whether the reference still resolved to the same data that the reference resolved to when the encapsulated data value with reference was created.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.integrityCheck",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "base64Binary",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/bin"
            ]
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.routeCode.originalText.integrityCheckAlgorithm",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.routeCode.originalText.integrityCheckAlgorithm",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Integrity Check Algorithm",
        "definition" : "Specifies the algorithm used to compute the integrityCheck value. The cryptographically strong checksum algorithm Secure Hash Algorithm-1 (SHA-1) is currently the industry standard. It has superseded the MD5 algorithm only a couple of years ago, when certain flaws in the security of MD5 were discovered. Currently the SHA-1 hash algorithm is the default choice for the integrity check algorithm. Note that SHA-256 is also entering widespread usage.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.integrityCheckAlgorithm",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "code",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/cs-simple"
            ]
          }
        ],
        "binding" : {
          "strength" : "required",
          "valueSet" : "http://terminology.hl7.org/ValueSet/v3-IntegrityCheckAlgorithm|2.0.0"
        }
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.routeCode.originalText.language",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.routeCode.originalText.language",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Language",

      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.routeCode.originalText.xmlText",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.routeCode.originalText.xmlText",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlText"
        ],
        "short" : "Allows for mixed text content. If @representation='B64', this SHALL be a base64binary string.",
        "definition" : "Data that is primarily intended for human interpretation or for further machine processing is outside the scope of HL7. This includes unformatted or formatted written language, multimedia data, or structured information as defined by a different standard (e.g., XML-signatures.)",
        "comment" : "This element is represented in XML as textual content. The actual name \"xmlText\" will not appear in a CDA instance.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.xmlText",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },

        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.routeCode.originalText.reference",
        "label" : "Reference",
        "definition" : "A telecommunication address (TEL), such as a URL for HTTP or FTP, which will resolve to precisely the same binary data that could as well have been provided as inline data.",
        "min" : 1,

        "base" : {

        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.routeCode.originalText.thumbnail",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.routeCode.originalText.thumbnail",
        "label" : "Thumbnail",
        "definition" : "An abbreviated rendition of the full data. A thumbnail requires significantly fewer resources than the full data, while still maintaining some distinctive similarity with the full data. A thumbnail is typically used with by-reference encapsulated data. It allows a user to select data more efficiently before actually downloading through the reference.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.thumbnail",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },

        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.approachSiteCode",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.approachSiteCode",
        "short" : "région anatomique d'administration :\nDans le cas où la posologie n'est pas structurée et décrite uniquement dans la partie narrative, cet élément ne doit pas être présent.   \nDans le cas où la posologie est structurée, si la région anatomique d'administration est connue, elle peut être indiquée.",
        "definition" : "région anatomique d'administration",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "*",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.approachSiteCode",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "*"

        ],

      {

        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.approachSiteCode.originalText.xmlText",

        ],
        "short" : "Allows for mixed text content. If @representation='B64', this SHALL be a base64binary string.",
        "definition" : "Data that is primarily intended for human interpretation or for further machine processing is outside the scope of HL7. This includes unformatted or formatted written language, multimedia data, or structured information as defined by a different standard (e.g., XML-signatures.)",
        "comment" : "This element is represented in XML as textual content. The actual name \"xmlText\" will not appear in a CDA instance.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",

      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.approachSiteCode.originalText.reference",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.approachSiteCode.originalText.reference",
        "label" : "Reference",
        "definition" : "A telecommunication address (TEL), such as a URL for HTTP or FTP, which will resolve to precisely the same binary data that could as well have been provided as inline data.",
        "min" : 1,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {

        },

      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.approachSiteCode.originalText.thumbnail",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.approachSiteCode.originalText.thumbnail",
        "label" : "Thumbnail",
        "definition" : "An abbreviated rendition of the full data. A thumbnail requires significantly fewer resources than the full data, while still maintaining some distinctive similarity with the full data. A thumbnail is typically used with by-reference encapsulated data. It allows a user to select data more efficiently before actually downloading through the reference.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.thumbnail",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },

        "min" : 0,

        ]

      {

        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.approachSiteCode.translation",
        "representation" : [
          "typeAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Translation",
        "definition" : "A set of other concept descriptors that translate this concept descriptor into other code systems.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "*",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "CD.translation",
          "min" : 0,

          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity",
        "short" : "Dose à administrer\u00a0: \r\n\nS'il n'y a pas de traitement, utiliser une valeur nullFlavor.\nS'il y a un traitement, les sous-éléments 'low' et 'high' permettent de fournir les doses minimales et maximales à administrer. Dans le cas où la dose est fixe, ces deux sous-éléments prendront les mêmes valeurs. Dans chaque élément 'low' et 'high', un élément 'translation' peut permettre de pointer sur l'élément de la partie narrative relative à cette information.\nDans le cas où l'on s'exprime en quantités indénombrables, l'unité doit être transmise. Les unités sont exprimées selon le système de codage UCUM.\nDans le cas où l'on s'exprime en quantités dénombrables (capsules, comprimés, gélules, etc.) l'unité ne doit pas être renseignée. A la place, on ajoute un champ 'translation' qui permet de pointer sur l'élément de la partie narrative relative à cette information.",
        "definition" : "Dose à administrer",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/IVL-PQ"
          }
        ],
        "mustSupport" : true
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity.nullFlavor",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity.nullFlavor",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Exceptional Value Detail",

        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {

      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity.low.translation.originalText.compression",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity.low.translation.originalText.compression",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Compression",
        "definition" : "Indicates whether the raw byte data is compressed, and what compression algorithm was used.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.compression",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "code",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/cs-simple"
            ]
          }
        ],
        "binding" : {
          "strength" : "required",
          "valueSet" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/ValueSet/CDACompressionAlgorithm"
        }
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity.low.translation.originalText.integrityCheck",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity.low.translation.originalText.integrityCheck",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Integrity Check",
        "definition" : "The integrity check is a short binary value representing a cryptographically strong checksum that is calculated over the binary data. The purpose of this property, when communicated with a reference is for anyone to validate later whether the reference still resolved to the same data that the reference resolved to when the encapsulated data value with reference was created.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.integrityCheck",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "base64Binary",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/bin"
            ]
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity.low.translation.originalText.integrityCheckAlgorithm",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity.low.translation.originalText.integrityCheckAlgorithm",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Integrity Check Algorithm",
        "definition" : "Specifies the algorithm used to compute the integrityCheck value. The cryptographically strong checksum algorithm Secure Hash Algorithm-1 (SHA-1) is currently the industry standard. It has superseded the MD5 algorithm only a couple of years ago, when certain flaws in the security of MD5 were discovered. Currently the SHA-1 hash algorithm is the default choice for the integrity check algorithm. Note that SHA-256 is also entering widespread usage.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.integrityCheckAlgorithm",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "code",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/cs-simple"
            ]
          }
        ],
        "binding" : {
          "strength" : "required",
          "valueSet" : "http://terminology.hl7.org/ValueSet/v3-IntegrityCheckAlgorithm|2.0.0"
        }
      },

      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity.high.translation.originalText.integrityCheck",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity.high.translation.originalText.integrityCheck",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Integrity Check",
        "definition" : "The integrity check is a short binary value representing a cryptographically strong checksum that is calculated over the binary data. The purpose of this property, when communicated with a reference is for anyone to validate later whether the reference still resolved to the same data that the reference resolved to when the encapsulated data value with reference was created.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.integrityCheck",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "base64Binary",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/bin"
            ]
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity.high.translation.originalText.integrityCheckAlgorithm",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity.high.translation.originalText.integrityCheckAlgorithm",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Integrity Check Algorithm",
        "definition" : "Specifies the algorithm used to compute the integrityCheck value. The cryptographically strong checksum algorithm Secure Hash Algorithm-1 (SHA-1) is currently the industry standard. It has superseded the MD5 algorithm only a couple of years ago, when certain flaws in the security of MD5 were discovered. Currently the SHA-1 hash algorithm is the default choice for the integrity check algorithm. Note that SHA-256 is also entering widespread usage.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.integrityCheckAlgorithm",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [

        "short" : "Allows for mixed text content. If @representation='B64', this SHALL be a base64binary string.",
        "definition" : "Data that is primarily intended for human interpretation or for further machine processing is outside the scope of HL7. This includes unformatted or formatted written language, multimedia data, or structured information as defined by a different standard (e.g., XML-signatures.)",
        "comment" : "This element is represented in XML as textual content. The actual name \"xmlText\" will not appear in a CDA instance.",

        "label" : "Thumbnail",
        "definition" : "An abbreviated rendition of the full data. A thumbnail requires significantly fewer resources than the full data, while still maintaining some distinctive similarity with the full data. A thumbnail is typically used with by-reference encapsulated data. It allows a user to select data more efficiently before actually downloading through the reference.",

      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity.high.inclusive",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.doseQuantity.high.inclusive",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "Specifies whether the limit is included in the interval (interval is closed) or excluded from the interval (interval is open).",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "IVXB_PQ.inclusive",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "boolean",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/bl-simple"
            ]
          }
        ],
        "defaultValueBoolean" : true
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity",
        "short" : "Rythme d'administration\u00a0: \r\n\nLe rythme d'administration permet d'indiquer la quantité de produit à administrer par unité de temps.\nLes sous-éléments 'low' et 'high' permettent de fournir le rythme minimal et maximal de l'administration. Dans le cas où le rythme est fixe, ces deux sous-éléments prendront les mêmes valeurs.\nL'argument @value permet d'indiquer la quantité de produit à administrer.\nL'argument @unit permet d'indiquer le rythme d'administration en combinant l'unité de quantité et l'unité de temps (séparés par le caractère ‘/'). Les unités sont exprimées selon le système de codage UCUM.\nDans chaque élément 'low' et 'high', un élément 'translation' peut permettre de pointer sur l'élément de la partie narrative relative à cette information.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/IVL-PQ"
          }
        ],
        "mustSupport" : true
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.nullFlavor",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.nullFlavor",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Exceptional Value Detail",
        "definition" : "If a value is an exceptional value (NULL-value), this specifies in what way and why proper information is missing.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ANY.nullFlavor",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [

              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/cs-simple"
            ]
          }

        }

        ],

        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Exceptional Value Detail",
        "definition" : "If a value is an exceptional value (NULL-value), this specifies in what way and why proper information is missing.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ANY.nullFlavor",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },

      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.originalText.compression",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.originalText.compression",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Compression",
        "definition" : "Indicates whether the raw byte data is compressed, and what compression algorithm was used.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.compression",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "code",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/cs-simple"
            ]
          }

          "strength" : "required",
          "valueSet" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/ValueSet/CDACompressionAlgorithm"
        }
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.originalText.integrityCheck",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.originalText.integrityCheck",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Integrity Check",
        "definition" : "The integrity check is a short binary value representing a cryptographically strong checksum that is calculated over the binary data. The purpose of this property, when communicated with a reference is for anyone to validate later whether the reference still resolved to the same data that the reference resolved to when the encapsulated data value with reference was created.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.integrityCheck",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "base64Binary",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/bin"
            ]
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.originalText.integrityCheckAlgorithm",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.originalText.integrityCheckAlgorithm",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Integrity Check Algorithm",
        "definition" : "Specifies the algorithm used to compute the integrityCheck value. The cryptographically strong checksum algorithm Secure Hash Algorithm-1 (SHA-1) is currently the industry standard. It has superseded the MD5 algorithm only a couple of years ago, when certain flaws in the security of MD5 were discovered. Currently the SHA-1 hash algorithm is the default choice for the integrity check algorithm. Note that SHA-256 is also entering widespread usage.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.integrityCheckAlgorithm",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "code",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/cs-simple"
            ]
          }
        ],
        "binding" : {
          "strength" : "required",
          "valueSet" : "http://terminology.hl7.org/ValueSet/v3-IntegrityCheckAlgorithm|2.0.0"
        }
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.originalText.language",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.originalText.language",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Language",
        "definition" : "For character based information the language property specifies the human language of the text.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.language",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"

            ]
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.originalText.mediaType",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.originalText.mediaType",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Media Type",
        "definition" : "Identifies the type of the encapsulated data and identifies a method to interpret or render the data.",

        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {

            ]
          }
        ],
        "binding" : {
          "strength" : "example",
          "valueSet" : "http://terminology.hl7.org/ValueSet/v3-MediaType"
        }
      },

        }
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.originalText.xmlText",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.originalText.xmlText",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlText"
        ],
        "short" : "Allows for mixed text content. If @representation='B64', this SHALL be a base64binary string.",
        "definition" : "Data that is primarily intended for human interpretation or for further machine processing is outside the scope of HL7. This includes unformatted or formatted written language, multimedia data, or structured information as defined by a different standard (e.g., XML-signatures.)",
        "comment" : "This element is represented in XML as textual content. The actual name \"xmlText\" will not appear in a CDA instance.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.xmlText",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "string",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/st-simple"
            ]
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.originalText.reference",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.originalText.reference",
        "label" : "Reference",
        "definition" : "A telecommunication address (TEL), such as a URL for HTTP or FTP, which will resolve to precisely the same binary data that could as well have been provided as inline data.",
        "min" : 1,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.reference",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/TEL"
          }
        ],
        "mustSupport" : true
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.originalText.thumbnail",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.originalText.thumbnail",
        "label" : "Thumbnail",
        "definition" : "An abbreviated rendition of the full data. A thumbnail requires significantly fewer resources than the full data, while still maintaining some distinctive similarity with the full data. A thumbnail is typically used with by-reference encapsulated data. It allows a user to select data more efficiently before actually downloading through the reference.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.thumbnail",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/ED"
          }
        ]
      },
      {

        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.qualifier",

        "max" : "0",
        "base" : {

          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "*"

          }

        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.translation.value",

          "xmlAttr"

        "definition" : "The magnitude of the measurement value in terms of the unit specified by this code.",

          "min" : 0,

      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.inclusive",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.low.inclusive",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "Specifies whether the limit is included in the interval (interval is closed) or excluded from the interval (interval is open).",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "IVXB_PQ.inclusive",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "boolean",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/bl-simple"
            ]
          }
        ],
        "defaultValueBoolean" : true
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.center",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.center",
        "label" : "Central Value",
        "definition" : "The arithmetic mean of the interval (low plus high divided by 2). The purpose of distinguishing the center as a semantic property is for conversions of intervals from and to point values.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "IVL_PQ.center",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/PQ"
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.width",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.width",
        "label" : "Width",
        "definition" : "The difference between high and low boundary. The purpose of distinguishing a width property is to handle all cases of incomplete information symmetrically. In any interval representation only two of the three properties high, low, and width need to be stated and the third can be derived.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "IVL_PQ.width",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/PQ"
          }
        ]
      },

      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.high.translation.originalText.compression",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.high.translation.originalText.compression",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Compression",
        "definition" : "Indicates whether the raw byte data is compressed, and what compression algorithm was used.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.compression",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "code",

        }
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.high.translation.originalText.integrityCheck",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.high.translation.originalText.integrityCheck",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Integrity Check",
        "definition" : "The integrity check is a short binary value representing a cryptographically strong checksum that is calculated over the binary data. The purpose of this property, when communicated with a reference is for anyone to validate later whether the reference still resolved to the same data that the reference resolved to when the encapsulated data value with reference was created.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.integrityCheck",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "base64Binary",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/bin"
            ]
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.high.translation.originalText.integrityCheckAlgorithm",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.high.translation.originalText.integrityCheckAlgorithm",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Integrity Check Algorithm",
        "definition" : "Specifies the algorithm used to compute the integrityCheck value. The cryptographically strong checksum algorithm Secure Hash Algorithm-1 (SHA-1) is currently the industry standard. It has superseded the MD5 algorithm only a couple of years ago, when certain flaws in the security of MD5 were discovered. Currently the SHA-1 hash algorithm is the default choice for the integrity check algorithm. Note that SHA-256 is also entering widespread usage.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.integrityCheckAlgorithm",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "code",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/cs-simple"
            ]
          }
        ],
        "binding" : {
          "strength" : "required",
          "valueSet" : "http://terminology.hl7.org/ValueSet/v3-IntegrityCheckAlgorithm|2.0.0"
        }
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.high.translation.originalText.language",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.high.translation.originalText.language",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Language",
        "definition" : "For character based information the language property specifies the human language of the text.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {

      },

            ]

        ],

      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.high.translation.originalText.xmlText",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.high.translation.originalText.xmlText",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlText"
        ],
        "short" : "Allows for mixed text content. If @representation='B64', this SHALL be a base64binary string.",
        "definition" : "Data that is primarily intended for human interpretation or for further machine processing is outside the scope of HL7. This includes unformatted or formatted written language, multimedia data, or structured information as defined by a different standard (e.g., XML-signatures.)",
        "comment" : "This element is represented in XML as textual content. The actual name \"xmlText\" will not appear in a CDA instance.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.xmlText",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },

            ]

        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.high.translation.originalText.reference",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.high.translation.originalText.reference",
        "label" : "Reference",
        "definition" : "A telecommunication address (TEL), such as a URL for HTTP or FTP, which will resolve to precisely the same binary data that could as well have been provided as inline data.",
        "min" : 1,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.reference",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [

          }
        ],
        "mustSupport" : true
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.high.translation.originalText.thumbnail",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.rateQuantity.high.translation.originalText.thumbnail",
        "label" : "Thumbnail",
        "definition" : "An abbreviated rendition of the full data. A thumbnail requires significantly fewer resources than the full data, while still maintaining some distinctive similarity with the full data. A thumbnail is typically used with by-reference encapsulated data. It allows a user to select data more efficiently before actually downloading through the reference.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ED.thumbnail",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },

          }
        ]
      },

        ],
        "definition" : "Specifies whether the limit is included in the interval (interval is closed) or excluded from the interval (interval is open).",
        "min" : 0,

        "base" : {

          "min" : 0,

          }
        ],
        "defaultValueBoolean" : true
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.maxDoseQuantity",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.maxDoseQuantity",
        "short" : "Dose maximale à administrer\u00a0: \r\n\nLa dose maximale à 'maxDoseQuantity' permet d'indiquer la quantité maximale de produit à administrer par unité de temps.\nPlusieurs occurrences de 'maxDoseQuantity' peuvent être utilisées pour indiquer différentes limites sur différentes périodes de temps.\nLes sous-éléments 'numerator' et 'denominator' permettent de fournir :\n- numerator : la quantité maximale à administrer,\n- denominator : la plage de temps sur laquelle s'applique ce maximum.\nLes unités sont exprimées selon le système de codage UCUM.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.maxDoseQuantity",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/RTO-PQ-PQ"
          }
        ],
        "mustSupport" : true
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.maxDoseQuantity.nullFlavor",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.maxDoseQuantity.nullFlavor",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Exceptional Value Detail",
        "definition" : "If a value is an exceptional value (NULL-value), this specifies in what way and why proper information is missing.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ANY.nullFlavor",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "code",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/cs-simple"
            ]
          }
        ],
        "binding" : {
          "strength" : "required",
          "valueSet" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/ValueSet/CDANullFlavor"
        }
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.maxDoseQuantity.numerator",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.maxDoseQuantity.numerator",
        "label" : "Numerator",
        "definition" : "The quantity that is being divided in the ratio. The default is the integer number 1 (one.)",
        "min" : 1,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "RTO_PQ_PQ.numerator",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/PQ"
          }
        ],
        "mustSupport" : true
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.maxDoseQuantity.denominator",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.maxDoseQuantity.denominator",
        "label" : "Denominator",
        "definition" : "The quantity that devides the numerator in the ratio. The default is the integer number 1 (one.) The denominator must not be zero.",
        "min" : 1,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "RTO_PQ_PQ.denominator",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/PQ"
          }
        ],
        "mustSupport" : true
      },

        "min" : 0,

        "definition" : "When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of constraints defined in an HL7-specified message type. This might be a common type (also known as CMET in the messaging communication environment), or content included within a wrapper. The value of this attribute provides a unique identifier for the type in question.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "InfrastructureRoot.typeId",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"

          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Assigning Authority Name",
        "definition" : "A human readable name or mnemonic for the assigning authority. The Assigning Authority Name has no computational value. The purpose of a Assigning Authority Name is to assist an unaided human interpreter of an II value to interpret the authority. Note: no automated processing must depend on the assigning authority name to be present in any form.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "II.assigningAuthorityName",
          "min" : 0,

      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.author:prescripteur",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.author",
        "sliceName" : "prescripteur",
        "short" : "Prescripteur : \r\n\nDans le cas où cette entrée Traitement prescrit est utilisée dans un document Prescription, cet élément ne doit pas être présent.\nDans le cas où cette entrée Traitement prescrit est utilisée dans un autre document, cet élément permet d'indiquer le prescripteur du traitement.",

      },

        "sliceName" : "auteurDuPrescription",
        "short" : "Auteur du document Prescription : \r\n\nDans le cas où cette entrée Traitement prescrit est utilisée dans un document Prescription, cet élément ne doit pas être présent.\nDans le cas où cette entrée Traitement prescrit est utilisée dans un autre document, cet élément permet d'indiquer l'auteur du document Prescription.\n",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",

      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frReferenceInterne",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship",
        "sliceName" : "frReferenceInterne",
        "short" : "Entrée Motif du traitement (Référence interne): \r\n\nLe motif du traitement (ou raison de l'administration) peut être indiqué en faisant référence à une autre entrée du document CDA constituant ce motif (par exemple une entrée Problème).\nDans l'élément 'act' de l'entryRelationship :\n- L'attribut @actCode='ACT'\n- L'attribut @moodCode='EVN'\n- L'élément 'id' de l'élément référencé est repris à l'identique dans les entrées à lier\n- L'élément 'code' doit concorder avec celui utilisé par l'élément de référence.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "*",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "*"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/EntryRelationship"
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frReferenceInterne.nullFlavor",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.nullFlavor",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Exceptional Value Detail",
        "definition" : "If a value is an exceptional value (NULL-value), this specifies in what way and why proper information is missing.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "ANY.nullFlavor",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "code",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/cs-simple"
            ]
          }
        ],
        "binding" : {
          "strength" : "required",
          "valueSet" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/ValueSet/CDANullFlavor"
        }
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frReferenceInterne.realmCode",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.realmCode",
        "definition" : "When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of realm-specific constraints. The value of this attribute identifies the realm in question",
        "min" : 0,

          }
        ]
      },

        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.typeId",
        "definition" : "When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of constraints defined in an HL7-specified message type. This might be a common type (also known as CMET in the messaging communication environment), or content included within a wrapper. The value of this attribute provides a unique identifier for the type in question.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "InfrastructureRoot.typeId",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [

          }
        ],
        "constraint" : [
          {
            "key" : "II-1",
            "severity" : "error",
            "human" : "An II instance must have either a root or an nullFlavor.",
            "expression" : "root.exists() or nullFlavor.exists()",
            "source" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/InfrastructureRoot"
          }
        ]
      },

        ],
        "label" : "Assigning Authority Name",
        "definition" : "A human readable name or mnemonic for the assigning authority. The Assigning Authority Name has no computational value. The purpose of a Assigning Authority Name is to assist an unaided human interpreter of an II value to interpret the authority. Note: no automated processing must depend on the assigning authority name to be present in any form.",
        "min" : 0,

      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frReferenceInterne.inversionInd",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.inversionInd",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "The entryRelationship.inversionInd can be set to \"true\" to indicate that the relationship should be interpreted as if the roles of the source and target entries were reversed.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "EntryRelationship.inversionInd",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },

          {
            "code" : "boolean",

        ]

      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frReferenceInterne.contextConductionInd",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.contextConductionInd",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "The entryRelationship.contextConductionInd differs from the otherwise common use of this attribute in that in all other cases where this attribute is used, the value is fixed at \"true\", whereas here the value is defaulted to \"true\", and can be changed to \"false\" when referencing an entry in the same document. Setting the context conduction to false when referencing an entry in the same document keeps clear the fact that the referenced object retains its original context.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "EntryRelationship.contextConductionInd",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "boolean",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/bl-simple"
            ]
          }
        ],
        "defaultValueBoolean" : true
      },
      {

      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frTraitementPrescritSubordonne",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship",
        "sliceName" : "frTraitementPrescritSubordonne",
        "short" : "Entrée Traitement prescrit subordonné : \r\n\nUne entrée FR-Traitement-prescrit de premier niveau peut contenir une ou plusieurs sous-entrées FR-Traitement-prescrit-subordonnee pour les cas spécifiques des dosages progressifs, fractionnés ou conditionnels, ou pour gérer la combinaison de médicaments.\nL'utilisation de sous-entrées FR-Traitement-prescrit-subordonnee pour traiter ces cas est facultative. Dans ce cas, l'information doit être fournie dans la partie narrative de l'entrée FR-Traitement-prescrit de premier niveau sous forme de texte libre.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "*",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "*"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/EntryRelationship"
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frTraitementPrescritSubordonne.nullFlavor",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.nullFlavor",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Exceptional Value Detail",

The value of this attribute provides a unique identifier for the type in question.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "InfrastructureRoot.typeId",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"

            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/II"
          }
        ],
        "constraint" : [

            "key" : "II-1",
            "severity" : "error",
            "human" : "An II instance must have either a root or an nullFlavor.",

          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Assigning Authority Name",
        "definition" : "A human readable name or mnemonic for the assigning authority. The Assigning Authority Name has no computational value. The purpose of a Assigning Authority Name is to assist an unaided human interpreter of an II value to interpret the authority. Note: no automated processing must depend on the assigning authority name to be present in any form.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",

        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.inversionInd",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "The entryRelationship.inversionInd can be set to \"true\" to indicate that the relationship should be interpreted as if the roles of the source and target entries were reversed.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "EntryRelationship.inversionInd",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"

        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frTraitementPrescritSubordonne.contextConductionInd",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.contextConductionInd",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "The entryRelationship.contextConductionInd differs from the otherwise common use of this attribute in that in all other cases where this attribute is used, the value is fixed at \"true\", whereas here the value is defaulted to \"true\", and can be changed to \"false\" when referencing an entry in the same document. Setting the context conduction to false when referencing an entry in the same document keeps clear the fact that the referenced object retains its original context.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "EntryRelationship.contextConductionInd",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "boolean",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/bl-simple"
            ]
          }
        ],
        "defaultValueBoolean" : true

          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "The entryRelationship.inversionInd can be set to \"true\" to indicate that the relationship should be interpreted as if the roles of the source and target entries were reversed.",

        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {

        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.contextConductionInd",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "The entryRelationship.contextConductionInd differs from the otherwise common use of this attribute in that in all other cases where this attribute is used, the value is fixed at \"true\", whereas here the value is defaulted to \"true\", and can be changed to \"false\" when referencing an entry in the same document. Setting the context conduction to false when referencing an entry in the same document keeps clear the fact that the referenced object retains its original context.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "EntryRelationship.contextConductionInd",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"

            "human" : "An II instance must have either a root or an nullFlavor.",

        "definition" : "A human readable name or mnemonic for the assigning authority. The Assigning Authority Name has no computational value.         "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.typeId",
        "definition" : "When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of constraints defined in an HL7-specified message type. This might be a common type (also known as CMET in the messaging communication environment), or content included within a wrapper. The value of this attribute provides a unique identifier for the type in question.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "InfrastructureRoot.typeId",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/II"
          }
        ],
        "constraint" : [
          {
            "key" : "II-1",
            "severity" : "error",
            "human" : "An II instance must have either a root or an nullFlavor.",
            "expression" : "root.exists() or nullFlavor.exists()",
            "source" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/InfrastructureRoot"
          }
        ]
      },

        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Assigning Authority Name",
        "definition" : "A human readable name or mnemonic for the assigning authority. The Assigning Authority Name has no computational value. The purpose of a Assigning Authority Name is to assist an unaided human interpreter of an II value to interpret the authority. Note: no automated processing must depend on the assigning authority name to be present in any form.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "II.assigningAuthorityName",
          "min" : 0,

          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "The entryRelationship.inversionInd can be set to \"true\" to indicate that the relationship should be interpreted as if the roles of the source and target entries were reversed.",

        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {

        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.contextConductionInd",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "The entryRelationship.contextConductionInd differs from the otherwise common use of this attribute in that in all other cases where this attribute is used, the value is fixed at \"true\", whereas here the value is defaulted to \"true\", and can be changed to \"false\" when referencing an entry in the same document. Setting the context conduction to false when referencing an entry in the same document keeps clear the fact that the referenced object retains its original context.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "EntryRelationship.contextConductionInd",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "boolean",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/bl-simple"
            ]
          }
        ],
        "defaultValueBoolean" : true
      },

          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "The entryRelationship.inversionInd can be set to \"true\" to indicate that the relationship should be interpreted as if the roles of the source and target entries were reversed.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "EntryRelationship.inversionInd",
          "min" : 0,

        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frQuantiteDeProduit.contextConductionInd",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.contextConductionInd",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "The entryRelationship.contextConductionInd differs from the otherwise common use of this attribute in that in all other cases where this attribute is used, the value is fixed at \"true\", whereas here the value is defaulted to \"true\", and can be changed to \"false\" when referencing an entry in the same document. Setting the context conduction to false when referencing an entry in the same document keeps clear the fact that the referenced object retains its original context.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "EntryRelationship.contextConductionInd",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },

            "code" : "boolean",
            "profile" : [

            ]
          }
        ],

        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.typeId",
        "definition" : "When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of constraints defined in an HL7-specified message type. This might be a common type (also known as CMET in the messaging communication environment), or content included within a wrapper. The value of this attribute provides a unique identifier for the type in question.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "InfrastructureRoot.typeId",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },

            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/II"
          }
        ],
        "constraint" : [
          {
            "key" : "II-1",
            "severity" : "error",
            "human" : "An II instance must have either a root or an nullFlavor.",
            "expression" : "root.exists() or nullFlavor.exists()",
            "source" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/InfrastructureRoot"
          }
        ]
      },

          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Assigning Authority Name",
        "definition" : "A human readable name or mnemonic for the assigning authority. The Assigning Authority Name has no computational value. The purpose of a Assigning Authority Name is to assist an unaided human interpreter of an II value to interpret the authority. Note: no automated processing must depend on the assigning authority name to be present in any form.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",

        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.typeId",
        "definition" : "When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of constraints defined in an HL7-specified message type. This might be a common type (also known as CMET in the messaging communication environment), or content included within a wrapper. The value of this attribute provides a unique identifier for the type in question.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "InfrastructureRoot.typeId",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/II"
          }
        ],
        "constraint" : [
          {
            "key" : "II-1",
            "severity" : "error",
            "human" : "An II instance must have either a root or an nullFlavor.",
            "expression" : "root.exists() or nullFlavor.exists()",
            "source" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/InfrastructureRoot"
          }
        ]
      },

          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Assigning Authority Name",
        "definition" : "A human readable name or mnemonic for the assigning authority. The Assigning Authority Name has no computational value. The purpose of a Assigning Authority Name is to assist an unaided human interpreter of an II value to interpret the authority. Note: no automated processing must depend on the assigning authority name to be present in any form.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",

          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "The entryRelationship.inversionInd can be set to \"true\" to indicate that the relationship should be interpreted as if the roles of the source and target entries were reversed.",

        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {

        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.contextConductionInd",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "The entryRelationship.contextConductionInd differs from the otherwise common use of this attribute in that in all other cases where this attribute is used, the value is fixed at \"true\", whereas here the value is defaulted to \"true\", and can be changed to \"false\" when referencing an entry in the same document. Setting the context conduction to false when referencing an entry in the same document keeps clear the fact that the referenced object retains its original context.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "EntryRelationship.contextConductionInd",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "boolean",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/bl-simple"
            ]
          }
        ],
        "defaultValueBoolean" : true
      },

        "definition" : "When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of realm-specific constraints. The value of this attribute identifies the realm in question",

      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frEnRapportAvecALD.typeId",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.typeId",
        "definition" : "When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of constraints defined in an HL7-specified message type. This might be a common type (also known as CMET in the messaging communication environment), or content included within a wrapper. The value of this attribute provides a unique identifier for the type in question.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "InfrastructureRoot.typeId",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/II"
          }
        ],
        "constraint" : [
          {
            "key" : "II-1",
            "severity" : "error",
            "human" : "An II instance must have either a root or an nullFlavor.",
            "expression" : "root.exists() or nullFlavor.exists()",
            "source" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/InfrastructureRoot"
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frEnRapportAvecALD.typeId.nullFlavor",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.typeId.nullFlavor",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Exceptional Value Detail",
        "definition" : "If a value is an exceptional value (NULL-value), this specifies in what way and why proper information is missing.",

        "base" : {

          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"

        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Assigning Authority Name",
        "definition" : "A human readable name or mnemonic for the assigning authority. The Assigning Authority Name has no computational value. The purpose of a Assigning Authority Name is to assist an unaided human interpreter of an II value to interpret the authority. Note: no automated processing must depend on the assigning authority name to be present in any form.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "II.assigningAuthorityName",

        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.inversionInd",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "The entryRelationship.inversionInd can be set to \"true\" to indicate that the relationship should be interpreted as if the roles of the source and target entries were reversed.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "EntryRelationship.inversionInd",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"

        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frEnRapportAvecALD.contextConductionInd",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.contextConductionInd",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "The entryRelationship.contextConductionInd differs from the otherwise common use of this attribute in that in all other cases where this attribute is used, the value is fixed at \"true\", whereas here the value is defaulted to \"true\", and can be changed to \"false\" when referencing an entry in the same document. Setting the context conduction to false when referencing an entry in the same document keeps clear the fact that the referenced object retains its original context.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "EntryRelationship.contextConductionInd",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "boolean",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/bl-simple"
            ]
          }
        ],
        "defaultValueBoolean" : true

      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frEnRapportAvecAccidentTravail.inversionInd",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.inversionInd",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "The entryRelationship.inversionInd can be set to \"true\" to indicate that the relationship should be interpreted as if the roles of the source and target entries were reversed.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "EntryRelationship.inversionInd",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "boolean",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/bl-simple"
            ]

        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frEnRapportAvecAccidentTravail.contextConductionInd",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.contextConductionInd",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "definition" : "The entryRelationship.contextConductionInd differs from the otherwise common use of this attribute in that in all other cases where this attribute is used, the value is fixed at \"true\", whereas here the value is defaulted to \"true\", and can be changed to \"false\" when referencing an entry in the same document. Setting the context conduction to false when referencing an entry in the same document keeps clear the fact that the referenced object retains its original context.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "EntryRelationship.contextConductionInd",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "boolean",
            "profile" : [
              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/bl-simple"
            ]
          }
        ],
        "defaultValueBoolean" : true
      },

        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.realmCode",
        "definition" : "When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of realm-specific constraints. The value of this attribute identifies the realm in question",

        "max" : "*",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "InfrastructureRoot.realmCode",

        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frEnRapportAvecLaPrevention.typeId",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.typeId",
        "definition" : "When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of constraints defined in an HL7-specified message type. This might be a common type (also known as CMET in the messaging communication environment), or content included within a wrapper. The value of this attribute provides a unique identifier for the type in question.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "InfrastructureRoot.typeId",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/II"
          }
        ],
        "constraint" : [
          {
            "key" : "II-1",
            "severity" : "error",
            "human" : "An II instance must have either a root or an nullFlavor.",
            "expression" : "root.exists() or nullFlavor.exists()",
            "source" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/InfrastructureRoot"
          }
        ]
      },
      {

        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.typeId.assigningAuthorityName",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Assigning Authority Name",
        "definition" : "A human readable name or mnemonic for the assigning authority. The Assigning Authority Name has no computational value. The purpose of a Assigning Authority Name is to assist an unaided human interpreter of an II value to interpret the authority. Note: no automated processing must depend on the assigning authority name to be present in any form.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "II.assigningAuthorityName",
          "min" : 0,

        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frNonRemboursable.realmCode",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.realmCode",
        "definition" : "When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of realm-specific constraints. The value of this attribute identifies the realm in question",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "*",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "InfrastructureRoot.realmCode",

        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frNonRemboursable.typeId",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.typeId",
        "definition" : "When valued in an instance, this attribute signals the imposition of constraints defined in an HL7-specified message type. This might be a common type (also known as CMET in the messaging communication environment), or content included within a wrapper. The value of this attribute provides a unique identifier for the type in question.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "InfrastructureRoot.typeId",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },
        "type" : [
          {
            "code" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/II"
          }
        ],
        "constraint" : [
          {
            "key" : "II-1",
            "severity" : "error",
            "human" : "An II instance must have either a root or an nullFlavor.",
            "expression" : "root.exists() or nullFlavor.exists()",
            "source" : "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/InfrastructureRoot"
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "id" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship:frNonRemboursable.typeId.nullFlavor",
        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.typeId.nullFlavor",

          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"

        "path" : "SubstanceAdministration.entryRelationship.typeId.assigningAuthorityName",
        "representation" : [
          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Assigning Authority Name",
        "definition" : "A human readable name or mnemonic for the assigning authority. The Assigning Authority Name has no computational value. The purpose of a Assigning Authority Name is to assist an unaided human interpreter of an II value to interpret the authority. Note: no automated processing must depend on the assigning authority name to be present in any form.",
        "min" : 0,
        "max" : "1",
        "base" : {
          "path" : "II.assigningAuthorityName",
          "min" : 0,
          "max" : "1"
        },

            "code" : "string",

              🔗 "http://hl7.org/cda/stds/core/StructureDefinition/st-simple"
            ]
          }
        ]

          "xmlAttr"
        ],
        "label" : "Displayable",
        "definition" : "Specifies if the identifier is intended for human display and data entry (displayable = true) as opposed to pure machine interoperation (displayable = false).",

        "max" : "1",
